# How To Make

## Strums
How do I make a multikey compatible noteskin? It's somewhat simple really! All you need is your normal strum notes, two middle strum notes, and an alt set of strum notes!

These are named:
* arrowLEFT 1, left confirm 1, and left press 1.
* arrowLEFT 2, left confirm 2, and left press 2.
* arrowDOWN 1, down confirm 1, and down press 1.
* arrowDOWN 2, down confirm 2, and down press 2.
* arrowUP 1, up confirm 1, and up press 1.
* arrowUP 2, up confirm 2, and up press 2.
* arrowRIGHT 1, right confirm 1, and right press 1.
* arrowRIGHT 2, right confirm 2, and right press 2.
* arrowSPACE 1, middle confirm 1, and middle press 1.
* arrowSPACE 2, middle confirm 2, and middle press 2.

## Notes
And of course you'll need to have notes, in the RGB format psych 1.0 uses. This decreases the amount of sprites we need to make overall!

These are named:
* left 1.
* left 2.
* down 1.
* down 2.
* up 1.
* up 2.
* right 1.
* right 2.
* middle 1.
* middle 2.

## Holds
Holds are special here, you have 2 sets to use, one for normal, and one for alt.

These are named:
* hold 1.
* hold 2.
* hold end 1.
* hold end 2.

Make sure the height of each set is the same! If not youll end up with some wonky looking holds!

## What to do with your noteskin
You can put the spritesheet and xml in your noteskin mod! It will find it automatically, as long as its in the same place as the normal 4k texture. This also works for noteType textures! Just stick it in the same place!

Your final spritesheet should look something like this:
![NoteSkinSprite](.github/images/spriteSheet.png)
